title: Object 18
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE: Object 18: "Kaboom-Box"}}__NOTOC__
== Description ==
'''Object 18''', colloquially called "'''Kaboom-Boxes'''", appear as silver or gray colored, retro boom-boxes commonly found in the 80s or 90s era. Similar to a regular boom-box, the amount of buttons and controls vary between individuals. While dials and knobs can appear on Kaboom-Boxes, they are non-functional and should be ignored. All Kaboom-Boxes are impervious to almost all known forms of damage, Because of this their density appears to be higher than normal. Higher density means carrying them with you can be troublesome in fast paced escape. On the back side of all Kaboom-Boxes found to date there is a logo for a company called "Death Metal Studios". It is currently unknown who or what exactly this organization is. Their only goal so far seems to be to produce Kaboom-Boxes and spread them about the Backrooms for wanderers to use. Their method of transporting their product is unknown. Investigation into their whereabouts and intentions is currently ongoing.
{{Blurimage|file=Oldboombox.jpg|hovertext=Kaboom-Box|caption=A Kaboom-Box in an unidentified level, photographer unknown|align=Right}}
Despite the chance for multiple buttons to appear on a Kaboom-Box, they all have the same function. When any button is pressed, the Kaboom-Box will activate and emit a loud tone between 50 Hz and 10,000 Hz and at a volume between 80 and 200 decibels. Due to the wide range of possible noises, effects vary from minor hearing loss to death. Via unknown methods, the user is unaffected by the noise emitted from the Kaboom-Box. After one use the Kaboom-Box will go into a "cool down period" lasting approximately 4 hours, using it again within this timeframe will result in a weak squeal and the Kaboom-Box breaking, becoming unusable. Examination of a broken Kaboom-Box has revealed that there are traces of [[Object 10|Whisper Shrooms]] inside and some form of dynamo or electrical generator linked to a striker. Cassette tapes can be found around the Backrooms and inserted into the Kaboom-Box, when activated after inserting a cassette tape, it will play a recording of what sounds like a radio broadcast instead of its usual effects. The broadcast changes between instances of the cassette tape, but all feature the same DJ named "Brian". These broadcasts often contain irrelevant or inconsistent information<ref>￼ While the broadcast can be heard playing songs, they are often garbled and nonsensical</ref>, such as weather reports, news, and possible entities in your area. While this information is usually false, exactly 53 seconds and 98 seconds into the cassette tape, it will cut to static and a deep monotone voice will start to speak. This voice is unnamed and gives similar information as Brian, but with more accuracy, such as the locations of useful items nearby or the locations of exits in your current level. It is unknown when this broadcast was recorded, or where it is or was based. It is unadvised to seek out the creators of this broadcast, as there is no information as to whether they are humans or entities.
{{Blurimage|align=Right|file=DeathMetalStudios.jpg.png|hovertext=Death Metal Studios logo|caption=The logo of Death Metal Studios}}
==Variants==
=== Black Kaboom-Box ===
A black Kaboom-Box looks and functions similarly to a normal Kaboom-box, but with black coloring. When activated, it will produce loud and agonizing screams which will attract any and all entities within 1,000 ft. It is highly discouraged to use this as a form of distraction unless you have a way to set it off remotely, as most entities will get there before you leave the area.

=== White Kaboom-Box ===
A white colored Kaboom-Box that has an opposite effect of the Black Kaboom-Box. When activated, all noise in a 30 ft radius around the user is canceled out, and replaced with a soft buzzing reminiscent of white noise. Any person or entity within the area of effect cannot hear anything except for the white noise produced by the kaboom box. This can be used for a total of 2 hours per day, resetting every 24 hours.

== Locations ==
Kaboom-Boxes can be found in 80s and 90s style levels such as [[Level 153]], [[Level 25]], residential levels such as [[Level 9]] and [[Level 11]], and can rarely be found in music themed levels.

== Death Metal Studios ==
Extremely little is known about this organization, only that they produce  the three known types of Kaboom-boxes. The cassette tapes are produced by a third party with no known association with Death Metal Studios. Investigation into the people/entities making up the group is to be handled by professionals. They are considered dangerous due to their production of black Kaboom-boxes.

== Discovery ==
The normal Kaboom-Box was initially discovered in 1976 by an unnamed wanderer, sightings of similar objects however, have been reported as far back as 1953. The first Black Kaboom-Box was found in 1987, on the decimated corpse of a wanderer found in [[Level 2]], it is assumed he tried to use it to defend himself and only attracted more entities.
